charts.use_when: Continuous values over ordered time with meaningful intermediate movement.
charts.avoid_when: There are only two time points or categories are not ordered.
charts.accessible_fallback: Direct-label each series and add a compact start/end/delta table.

charts.use_when: Comparing magnitudes across a bounded set of categories.
charts.avoid_when: The slide needs to show a continuous distribution or more than twelve categories.
charts.accessible_fallback: Sort bars and print values at bar ends.

charts.use_when: Precise comparison matters and bars would consume too much ink.
charts.avoid_when: The audience expects part-to-whole area or category labels are extremely long.
charts.accessible_fallback: Use one aligned value column beside the dots.

charts.use_when: A small number of stable parts must be compared across a few totals.
charts.avoid_when: More than five parts need precise cross-category comparison.
charts.accessible_fallback: Add total labels and a companion table for exact segment values.

charts.use_when: Showing the shape, spread, skew, or modes of continuous observations.
charts.avoid_when: There are too few observations or bins would imply false precision.
charts.accessible_fallback: Annotate median and range and provide sample count.

charts.use_when: Comparing spread and outliers across several groups for a data-literate audience.
charts.avoid_when: The audience is unfamiliar with quartiles or the sample is very small.
charts.accessible_fallback: Add median, interquartile range, and sample-size labels.

charts.use_when: Showing relationship, clusters, and outliers between two quantitative variables.
charts.avoid_when: The claim is causal or overplotting hides the distribution.
charts.accessible_fallback: Label important points and state correlation limitations in the caption.

charts.use_when: The estimate and its uncertainty or target range are both decision-relevant.
charts.avoid_when: Intervals have different definitions or confidence levels.
charts.accessible_fallback: Print estimate and interval bounds in aligned text.

charts.use_when: A small number of measurable transfers or losses between named stages matters.
charts.avoid_when: Values are unavailable, paths cross heavily, or direction is the only message.
charts.accessible_fallback: Use a numbered flow with explicit quantities at each transition.

charts.use_when: Sequence, duration, milestones, and dependencies are the claim.
charts.avoid_when: Dates are uncertain enough that a roadmap would imply false commitment.
charts.accessible_fallback: Add a dated milestone list in reading order.

charts.use_when: Location, distance, region, route, or spatial concentration is essential to the claim.
charts.avoid_when: Population or area size would visually distort a non-geographic comparison.
charts.accessible_fallback: Add a ranked region table and label every highlighted location.

charts.use_when: The audience must retrieve exact values or compare many attributes.
charts.avoid_when: A single visual pattern or trend is the real message.
charts.accessible_fallback: The table is already the fallback; highlight only decision-relevant cells.
